attachment.content:
- make: Run the project Makefile (regenerates Xcode project via xcodegen and opens it).
- pr: Wrap current uncommitted changes into a Feature/RAD-XXXX branch and open a Bitbucket PR.
- post-merge: Sync main after a PR has been merged (git checkout main + pull --ff-only + make).
- pdf-viewer:open: Open a PDF in the interactive viewer
- pdf-viewer:fill-form: Fill PDF form fields interactively with live visual feedback
- pdf-viewer:sign: Place a signature or initials image on a PDF
- pdf-viewer:annotate: Collaboratively annotate a PDF — propose markup, review together, iterate
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:extract-design: Extract the full design language from any website URL. Produces 8 output files including AI-optimized markdown, visual HTML preview, Tailwind config, React theme, shadcn/ui theme, Figma variables, W3C design tokens, and CSS variables. Also runs WCAG accessibility scoring. Use when user says 'extract design', 'get design system', 'design language', 'design tokens', 'what colors/fonts does this site use', or '/extract-design'.
- pdf-viewer:view-pdf: Interactive PDF viewer. Use when the user wants to open, show, or view a PDF and collaborate on it visually — annotate, highlight, stamp, fill form fields, place signature/initials, or review markup together. Not for summarization or text extraction (use native Read instead).
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Issue RADIACODE-IOS-R in **radiacode**

**Description**: App Hang Fully Blocked: App hanging between 8.1 and 8.9 seconds.
**Culprit**: closure in LocationManager.init
**First Seen**: 2026-05-16T09:07:37.598Z
**Last Seen**: 2026-05-18T17:43:08.000Z
**Occurrences**: 2
**Users Impacted**: 2
**Status**: unresolved
**Substatus**: new
**Issue Type**: error
**Issue Category**: error
**Platform**: cocoa
**Project**: radiacode-ios
**URL**: https://radiacode.sentry.io/issues/RADIACODE-IOS-R

## Event Details

**Event ID**: 672befe3c0a84bd3a158383af324a477
**Type**: error
**Occurred At**: 2026-05-18T17:43:08.170Z

### Error

```
App Hang Fully Blocked: App hanging between 8.1 and 8.9 seconds.
```

**Most Relevant Frame:**
─────────────────────
    at closure in LocationManager.init (LocationManager.swift:38)

    35 │             locationManager.distanceFilter = 2
    36 │             
    37 │             self.locationManager = locationManager
  → 38 │             self.authorizationStatus = locationManager.authorizationStatus
    39 │             
    40 │             if authorizationStatus.isAvailable {
    41 │                 updateCurrentLocation()

**Full Stacktrace:**
────────────────
```
    at swift::runJobInEstablishedExecutorContext (<unknown>)
    at closure in LocationManager.init (LocationManager.swift:38)
            self.authorizationStatus = locationManager.authorizationStatus
    at CLClientCreateWithBundleIdentifierAndPathWithWebsiteOnSilo (<unknown>)
    at CLClientCreateWithBundleIdentifierAndPathWithWebsiteOnSilo (<unknown>)
    at CLClientCreateWithBundleIdentifierAndPathWithWebsiteOnSilo (<unknown>)
    at _NSXPCDistantObjectSimpleMessageSend3 (<unknown>)
    at -[NSXPCConnection _sendSelector:withProxy:arg1:arg2:arg3:] (<unknown>)
    at -[NSXPCConnection _sendInvocation:orArguments:count:methodSignature:selector:withProxy:] (<unknown>)
    at __NSXPCCONNECTION_IS_WAITING_FOR_A_SYNCHRONOUS_REPLY__ (<unknown>)
    at xpc_connection_send_message_with_reply_sync (<unknown>)
    at dispatch_mach_send_with_result_and_wait_for_reply (<unknown>)
    at _dispatch_mach_send_and_wait_for_reply (<unknown>)
    at mach_msg (<unknown>)
    at mach_msg_overwrite (<unknown>)
    at mach_msg2_internal (<unknown>)
    at mach_msg2_trap (<unknown>)
```

### User

content.thinking:
The issue is an App Hang in `LocationManager.init` at line 38, where `self.authorizationStatus = locationManager.authorizationStatus` is causing a synchronous XPC call that blocks the main thread for 8-9 seconds.

Looking at the stacktrace:
- The hang is triggered by accessing `locationManager.authorizationStatus`
- This makes a synchronous XPC call via `CLClientCreateWithBundleIdentifierAndPathWithWebsiteOnSilo` 
- Which internally does `xpc_connection_send_message_with_reply_sync` — a blocking synchronous call


- The call happens within a Swift concurrency executor context, so accessing the authorization status from this context triggers a synchronous IPC call to the location daemon that takes 8+ seconds to respond.

Let me examine the LocationManager.swift file to see how this is being initialized.

content.text: The issue is an **App Hang** in `LocationManager.init` at line 38 — accessing `locationManager.authorizationStatus` triggers a synchronous XPC call to the location daemon (`locationd`) that blocks for 8–9 seconds. Let me look at the file.

input.prompt: Find the file LocationManager.swift in /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios. Return its full path and full contents.

content.text:
Found it! Here are the details:

**Full Path:** `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode/Services/LocationManager/LocationManager.swift`

**Full Contents:**

The file is a 146-line Swift file that implements the `LocationManager` class. It's an observable object that manages CoreLocation functionality with the following key features:

- **Published properties** for authorization status, last known location, and heading
- **Initialization** sets up `CLLocationManager` on the main thread with navigation-level accuracy and background updates enabled
- **Authorization flow** automatically escalates from "whenInUse" to "always" authorization
- **Location tracking methods**: `updateCurrentLocation()`, `startTracking()`, and `stopTracking()`
- **Async location retrieval** via `currentLocation()` using checked throwing continuations
- **Delegate implementation** handling location updates, heading changes, authorization changes, and errors
- **Extensions** for:
  - `CLAuthorizationStatus` to check if location access is available
  - `CLLocation` to convert to a `Coordinates` type

The implementation includes Russian comments explaining the main thread requirement for `CLLocationManager` and the "warm-up" of the authorization status value.

toolUseResult.prompt: Find the file LocationManager.swift in /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios. Return its full path and full contents.

content.text:
Found it! Here are the details:

**Full Path:** `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode/Services/LocationManager/LocationManager.swift`

**Full Contents:**

The file is a 146-line Swift file that implements the `LocationManager` class. It's an observable object that manages CoreLocation functionality with the following key features:

- **Published properties** for authorization status, last known location, and heading
- **Initialization** sets up `CLLocationManager` on the main thread with navigation-level accuracy and background updates enabled
- **Authorization flow** automatically escalates from "whenInUse" to "always" authorization
- **Location tracking methods**: `updateCurrentLocation()`, `startTracking()`, and `stopTracking()`
- **Async location retrieval** via `currentLocation()` using checked throwing continuations
- **Delegate implementation** handling location updates, heading changes, authorization changes, and errors
- **Extensions** for:
  - `CLAuthorizationStatus` to check if location access is available
  - `CLLocation` to convert to a `Coordinates` type

The implementation includes Russian comments explaining the main thread requirement for `CLLocationManager` and the "warm-up" of the authorization status value.

content.thinking: The Explore agent didn't give me the full file contents. Let me read the file directly.

Now I can see the issue clearly. The problem is on line 38:

```swift
self.authorizationStatus = locationManager.authorizationStatus
```

This is inside a `Task { @MainActor in ... }` closure. On first access, `locationManager.authorizationStatus` triggers a synchronous XPC call to `locationd`, which can take a long time (8-9 seconds in the crash report). This is the "warm-up" (прогрев) the comment on line 29 mentions.

The issue is that accessing `.authorizationStatus` for the first time forces CoreLocation to synchronously communicate with the `locationd` daemon via XPC, which on some devices or in some states can take several seconds. 

Since the delegate is already set on line 30, I don't need to manually read `authorizationStatus` at all — the `locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization` callback will fire automatically on the main thread once the status is determined, so I can simply remove line 38 and let the delegate handle populating the value. значение определяется некорректно" (Warm-up of the initial authorizationStatus value, without it the value is determined incorrectly). This suggests someone added this line to work around an issue where the delegate wasn't being called initially.

Actually, looking at Apple docs: `locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization(_:)` is called when the app creates the location manager AND when authorization changes. So when `locationManager.delegate = self` is set on line 30, this delegate method should be called immediately (on next run loop iteration) with the current authorization status. So we don't need line 38.

Wait, actually the delegate is called after the `CLLocationManager` is created and the delegate is set. The `locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization` method is called automatically. So removing line 38 should be safe, and `authorizationStatus` will be populated by the delegate callback.

But wait — what if the delegate callback happens asynchronously? In that case, there's a brief moment when `authorizationStatus` is `.notDetermined` even if it was previously granted, which would trigger the Combine subscription to request authorization unnecessarily. However, `locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization` is called synchronously on the main thread right after the location manager is created, so this window is minimal. The real issue is that the Combine subscription gets set up in `init()` before the delegate callback fires, meaning `authorizationStatus` starts as `.notDetermined` and triggers the authorization request prematurely.

The solution is straightforward: remove line 38 and let the delegate handle the initial status update. Even if `requestWhenInUseAuthorization()` gets called when authorization is already granted, it's a no-op, so there's no harm.

Actually, I need to think about the timing more carefully. The `Task { @MainActor in }` runs the closure on the main actor, and since `init()` might not be on the main actor, the task execution gets scheduled for later.

The Combine subscription is set up in `init()` before the task runs, so when `locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization` fires (from the delegate), it updates `authorizationStatus`, triggering the Combine pipeline to handle the status correctly.

So the cleanest fix is to remove line 38 entirely and trust the delegate to provide the initial status. — no XPC hang since the system already has the value cached. The `updateCurrentLocation()` call can stay in the delegate callback or rely on the existing Combine subscription that triggers it when authorization changes, so the real fix is just removing lines 38-42 to eliminate the synchronous warm-up read.

content.text:
Found the bug. Line 38 — `locationManager.authorizationStatus` — forces a **synchronous XPC call** to the `locationd` daemon on first access, which can take 8+ seconds on some devices. The comment even calls it a "warm-up" (прогрев).

The fix is to **remove lines 38–42** entirely. The delegate method `locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization` (line 113) already sets `authorizationStatus` when the system delivers the value asynchronously, and the Combine subscription (lines 45–63) already calls `updateCurrentLocation()` when the status becomes available. No warm-up needed.

```swift
// REMOVE:
self.authorizationStatus = locationManager.authorizationStatus

if authorizationStatus.isAvailable {
    updateCurrentLocation()
}
```

After the fix, `init` becomes:

```swift
Task { @MainActor in
    let locationManager = CLLocationManager()
    locationManager.delegate = self
    locationManager.desiredAccuracy = kCLLocationAccuracyBestForNavigation
    locationManager.allowsBackgroundLocationUpdates = true
    locationManager.showsBackgroundLocationIndicator = true
    locationManager.pausesLocationUpdatesAutomatically = false
    locationManager.distanceFilter = 2
    self.locationManager = locationManager
    // authorizationStatus is set by locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization delegate callback
    // updateCurrentLocation() is called by the Combine subscription when status becomes available
}
```

The delegate fires shortly after the manager is created and delivers the already-known authorization status without any IPC round-trip — exactly the same value, just without blocking the main thread.

Want me to apply this change?

content.content:
1	# Memory
2	
3	- [No worktrees](feedback_no_worktrees.md) — work in the main repo path, not isolated worktrees.
4	- [Jira and Bitbucket coordinates](reference_jira_bitbucket.md) — Jira URL, repo, auth env vars, ticket workflow for RAD-XXXX requests.
5	- [Do not delete local branches](feedback_no_local_branch_delete.md) — leave merged Feature/RAD-XXXX branches in place after pull.
6	- [/post-merge routine](feedback_post_merge_routine.md) — `git checkout main && git pull --ff-only && make` after a PR is merged.
7	- [/pr-XXXX command](feedback_pr_command.md) — wrap current uncommitted changes into a Feature/RAD-XXXX branch and open a Bitbucket PR.
8	- [Propose before applying](feedback_propose_before_apply.md) — show code in chat for "how to" / "I'd like" prompts; only edit on explicit "применить"/"сделай".
9	- [No force unwrap](feedback_no_force_unwrap.md) — never propose Swift code with `!` (force unwrap, `try!`, `as!`); use safe alternatives.
10	- [Device protocol reference](reference_device_protocol.md) — RadiaCode USB/Bluetooth protocol at `docs/Protocol.pdf` (287 pp), also documented in CLAUDE.md.
11	- [No Co-Authored-By](feedback_no_coauthor.md) — omit `Co-Authored-By: Claude ...` trailer in commits.
12	- [No guesswork](feedback_no_guesswork.md) — никогда не реализовывать по догадкам; читать Protocol.pdf из вложений Jira-тикета.
13

# Memory

- [No worktrees](feedback_no_worktrees.md) — work in the main repo path, not isolated worktrees.
- [Jira and Bitbucket coordinates](reference_jira_bitbucket.md) — Jira URL, repo, auth env vars, ticket workflow for RAD-XXXX requests.
- [Do not delete local branches](feedback_no_local_branch_delete.md) — leave merged Feature/RAD-XXXX branches in place after pull.
- [/post-merge routine](feedback_post_merge_routine.md) — `git checkout main && git pull --ff-only && make` after a PR is merged.
- [/pr-XXXX command](feedback_pr_command.md) — wrap current uncommitted changes into a Feature/RAD-XXXX branch and open a Bitbucket PR.
- [Propose before applying](feedback_propose_before_apply.md) — show code in chat for "how to" / "I'd like" prompts; only edit on explicit "применить"/"сделай".
- [No force unwrap](feedback_no_force_unwrap.md) — never propose Swift code with `!` (force unwrap, `try!`, `as!`); use safe alternatives.
- [Device protocol reference](reference_device_protocol.md) — RadiaCode USB/Bluetooth protocol at `docs/Protocol.pdf` (287 pp), also documented in CLAUDE.md.
- [No Co-Authored-By](feedback_no_coauthor.md) — omit `Co-Authored-By: Claude ...` trailer in commits.
- [No guesswork](feedback_no_guesswork.md) — никогда не реализовывать по догадкам; читать Protocol.pdf из вложений Jira-тикета.

---
name: respond-in-russian
description: Always respond in Russian, regardless of the language of the user's message.
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

Всегда отвечать на русском — даже если вопрос задан на английском или другом языке.

**Why:** Пользователь работает на русском и ожидает ответы на русском.

**How to apply:** Любое сообщение в чате — код-ревью, анализ багов, пояснения, вопросы — всё на русском. Исключение: фрагменты кода и технические идентификаторы остаются как есть.

content.text:
Сохранено. Теперь буду всегда отвечать на русском.

Применить исправление бага с `LocationManager`?

content.thinking: The user wants me to rewrite everything I said above in Russian. Let me summarize the Sentry issue analysis and the fix in Russian.

content.text:
**Sentry issue RADIACODE-IOS-R — App Hang в `LocationManager.init`**

Ошибка — это зависание приложения на 8–9 секунд. Источник — строка 38 в [LocationManager.swift](RadiaCode/Services/LocationManager/LocationManager.swift:38):

```swift
self.authorizationStatus = locationManager.authorizationStatus
```

При первом обращении к `.authorizationStatus` CoreLocation делает **синхронный XPC-вызов** к демону `locationd`. На некоторых устройствах этот вызов занимает несколько секунд, блокируя главный поток — отсюда App Hang. В стектрейсе это хорошо видно: `__NSXPCCONNECTION_IS_WAITING_FOR_A_SYNCHRONOUS_REPLY__` → `xpc_connection_send_message_with_reply_sync` → `mach_msg2_trap`.

Ирония в том, что комментарий на строке 29 сам описывает это как «прогрев» — то есть проблема была известна, но решена неудачно.

---

**Исправление: удалить строки 38–42 целиком.**

Они не нужны, потому что:
- Делегатный метод `locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization` (строка 113) уже обновляет `authorizationStatus` — система сама его вызовет после создания менеджера, и без синхронного IPC.
- Combine-подписка (строки 45–63) уже вызывает `updateCurrentLocation()` когда статус становится доступным.

После правки `Task { @MainActor in }` выглядит так:

```swift
Task { @MainActor in
    let locationManager = CLLocationManager()
    locationManager.delegate = self
    locationManager.desiredAccuracy = kCLLocationAccuracyBestForNavigation
    locationManager.allowsBackgroundLocationUpdates = true
    locationManager.showsBackgroundLocationIndicator = true
    locationManager.pausesLocationUpdatesAutomatically = false
    locationManager.distanceFilter = 2
    self.locationManager = locationManager
}
```

Применить?
